code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a fluent, single sentence.
    Uses predicate‑specific phrasing and combines clauses with proper conjunctions.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Predicate‑specific templates
    def render(triple: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Simple mapping for better readability
        templates = {
            "discoverer": f"{o} discovered {s}",
            "epoch": f"{s} has an epoch of {o}",
            "apoapsis": f"{s} reaches an apoapsis of {o}",
            "periapsis": f"{s} reaches a periapsis of {o}",
            "averageSpeed": f"{s} moves at an average speed of {o}",
            "escapeVelocity": f"{s} has an escape velocity of {o}",
            "mass": f"{s} has a mass of {o}",
            "temperature": f"{s} has a temperature of {o}",
            "density": f"{s} has a density of {o}",
            "rotationPeriod": f"{s} rotates every {o} units",
            "maximumTemperature": f"{s} reaches a maximum temperature of {o}",
            "meanTemperature": f"{s} has a mean temperature of {o}",
            "minimumTemperature": f"{s} reaches a minimum temperature of {o}",
            "formerName": f"{s} was formerly known as {o}",
            "absoluteMagnitude": f"{s} has an absolute magnitude of {o}",
            "discovered": f"{s} was discovered on {o}",
            "surfaceArea": f"{s} has a surface area of {o}",
            "deathCause": f"{s} died of {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"{s} died in {o}",
            "nationality": f"{s} was {o}",
            "stateOfOrigin": f"{s} originated from {o}",
            "almaMater": f"{s} studied at {o}",
            "birthPlace": f"{s} was born in {o}",
            "doctoralStudent": f"{s} supervised {o}",
            "deathDate": f"{s} died on {o}",
            "birthDate": f"{s} was born on {o}",
        }
        return templates.get(p, f"The {p} of {s} is {o}")

    clauses = [render(t) for t in triples]

    # Combine clauses with commas and an 'and' before the last one
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a single fluent sentence.
      Each triple is rendered as a clause and the clauses are combined with commas.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
      clauses = []
      for triple in triples:
          # Basic phrasing – can be extended for specific predicates later
          clause = f"The {triple.predicate} of {triple.subject} is {triple.object}"
          clauses.append(clause)
      # Join all clauses into one sentence
      return ", ".join(clauses) + "."
